name: PipelineTerraformInstaller
description: Find in cache or download a specific version of Terraform and prepend it to the PATH
author: sethbacon

inputs.name: binary
inputs.label: Binary
inputs.helpMarkDown: Which IaC binary to install: Terraform (HashiCorp) or OpenTofu.

inputs.name: terraformVersion
inputs.label: Version
inputs.helpMarkDown: The version to install. Use 'latest' to install the current latest release. Applies to both Terraform and OpenTofu.

inputs.name: downloadSource
inputs.label: Download source
inputs.helpMarkDown: Where to download the Terraform binary from. OpenTofu always downloads from GitHub releases.

inputs.name: registryUrl
inputs.label: Registry base URL
inputs.helpMarkDown: Base URL of your terraform-registry-backend instance. Must use HTTPS. Example: https://registry.example.com

inputs.name: registryMirrorName
inputs.label: Mirror configuration name
inputs.helpMarkDown: The mirror configuration name in your registry backend. This is the {name} path segment in /terraform/binaries/{name}/...

inputs.name: registryAllowedHosts
inputs.label: Allowed download_url hosts
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional comma- or newline-separated allowlist of hostnames the registry's pre-signed download_url is permitted to use (e.g. storage.example.com, or *.s3.amazonaws.com to allow any subdomain). When empty (default), the download_url host is trusted as returned by the registry. Set this to pin a compromised or misbehaving registry from redirecting downloads to an arbitrary HTTPS host.

inputs.name: mirrorBaseUrl
inputs.label: Mirror base URL
inputs.helpMarkDown: Base URL of your binary mirror. Must use HTTPS. Must serve files at the same path structure as releases.hashicorp.com/terraform. Example: https://artifacts.example.com/hashicorp/terraform

inputs.name: requireGpgSignature
inputs.label: Require GPG signature verification
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled, fail if the GPG signature file is unavailable. Defaults to true for HashiCorp downloads. Disable for mirrors that do not serve .sig files.

inputs.name: requireCosignVerification
inputs.label: Require cosign signature verification
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled (default), fail the install if cosign is not available on the agent or the OpenTofu SHA256SUMS signature cannot be verified against OpenTofu's pinned Sigstore identity. Install cosign on the agent (e.g. sigstore/cosign-installer) to satisfy this. Set to false only to fall back to checksum-only verification on agents without cosign.

inputs.name: cosignSha256
inputs.label: Pinned cosign binary SHA256 (optional)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional. cosign is discovered via a PATH lookup and is never itself integrity-verified by default, so a prior step that prepends a directory to PATH could shadow it with a stub that always reports success. If you provision cosign from a known, integrity-verified source, set this to its exact SHA256 to pin and verify the resolved binary before it is trusted -- a mismatch fails the install. Leave empty (default) to keep the existing ambient-PATH trust behavior unchanged.

inputs.name: requireChecksum
inputs.label: Require checksum verification
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled (default), fail if the mirror or registry does not serve a SHA256SUMS file for the requested version, matching the PolicyAgentInstaller and TerraformDocsInstaller defaults (HashiCorp and OpenTofu releases always publish checksums, so fresh installs from those sources are unaffected). Also controls tool-cache re-verification on persistent/self-hosted agents: when enabled, a cached tool that carries no local integrity marker (cached by an older installer version, or by a job that ran with verification disabled) is re-downloaded and re-verified before use, degrading to a warning only when the source is unreachable (offline/air-gapped agents keep working). Avoid mixing requireChecksum values across jobs that share an agent's tool cache. Set to false to skip both checks.

outputVariables.name: terraformLocation
outputVariables.description: The location of the terraform binary that was installed on the agent.

outputVariables.name: terraformDownloadedFrom
outputVariables.description: The source from which terraform was downloaded: 'hashicorp', 'registry:<url>', or 'mirror:<url>'.

messages.InputVersionNotValidSemanticVersion: Input version %s is not a valid semantic version
messages.TerraformDownloadFailed: Failed to download binary from url %s. Error: %s
messages.TerraformVersionNotFound: Unable to get latest version from source, using fallback version
messages.GettingLatestOpenTofuVersion: Getting latest OpenTofu version from GitHub releases.
messages.InsecureUrlRejected: Insecure URL rejected: %s. Only HTTPS URLs are allowed.
messages.RegistryRequestFailed: Registry request failed for %s. HTTP status: %s
messages.ResolvingLatestFromRegistry: Resolving latest Terraform version from registry: %s
messages.RegistryDownloadHostNotAllowed: Registry download_url host '%s' is not in registryAllowedHosts: %s
messages.RegistryDownloadHostIsPrivate: Registry download_url host '%s' is a private/link-local address, which is refused by default (a common SSRF target, e.g. the cloud metadata service). Set registryAllowedHosts to explicitly allow this host if it is a legitimate internal mirror.
messages.CachedToolVerificationFailed: Cached %s failed integrity re-verification (stored: %s, actual: %s). The cached copy may have been tampered with or corrupted since it was last verified; clear the tool cache for this version and re-run to force a fresh, verified download.
messages.ReverifyingCachedTool: Cache hit for %s has no stored integrity marker (cached before re-verification existed, or with checksum verification disabled). Re-downloading the release to re-verify the cached executable; set requireChecksum to false to skip this.
messages.CachedToolReverificationUnavailable: Could not re-verify cached %s: %s. Proceeding with the cached executable — expected for offline/air-gapped agents; set requireChecksum to false to skip the attempt and silence this warning.
messages.CachedToolReverificationMismatch: Cached %s does not match the freshly downloaded, verified release (verified: %s, cached: %s). The cached copy was either never verified or has been altered since caching; clear the agent's tool cache for this version and re-run.
messages.CachedToolReverified: Cached %s re-verified against the freshly downloaded, verified release; integrity marker written for future cache hits.
